name: Notion
description: Search, create, and update Notion pages and databases. Manage workspace content, query databases with filters, and append rich content blocks.
author: DenPaq

tools.name: notion_search
tools.description: Search for pages and databases in the Notion workspace by title. Returns matching pages and databases the integration has access to.

query.description: Search query to match against page and database titles.

filter_type.description: Filter results by type: 'page' or 'database'. Omit for both.

page_size.description: Number of results to return (max 100). Defaults to 10.

tools.name: notion_get_page
tools.description: Get a Notion page's properties and content blocks. Returns the page title, properties, and child blocks (text, headings, lists, etc.).

page_id.description: The Notion page ID (UUID format, with or without dashes).

tools.name: notion_create_page
tools.description: Create a new page in Notion. Can be a child of another page or a new item in a database. Requires human approval.

parent_id.description: Parent page ID or database ID where the new page will be created.

properties.description: JSON string of database properties to set (only for database parents). Example: {"Status": {"select": {"name": "In Progress"}}}

content.description: Markdown-like content for the page body. Supports headings (## H2), bullets (- item), numbered lists (1. item), bold (**text**), italic (*text*), code (`code`), and plain paragraphs.

tools.name: notion_update_page
tools.description: Update a Notion page's properties (title, status, tags, etc.). Does not modify page content blocks — use notion_append_blocks for that.

page_id.description: The Notion page ID to update.

properties.description: JSON string of properties to update. Example: {"Status": {"select": {"name": "Done"}}, "Priority": {"select": {"name": "High"}}}

tools.name: notion_archive_page
tools.description: Archive (soft-delete) a Notion page. The page can be restored from trash. Requires human approval.

page_id.description: The Notion page ID to archive.

tools.name: notion_get_database
tools.description: Get a Notion database's schema — its title, description, and property definitions (columns). Useful for understanding the structure before querying or creating items.

tools.name: notion_query_database
tools.description: Query a Notion database with optional filters and sorts. Returns matching rows (pages) with their properties.

database_id.description: The Notion database ID to query.

filter.description: JSON string of Notion filter object. Example: {"property": "Status", "select": {"equals": "In Progress"}}

sorts.description: JSON string of sorts array. Example: [{"property": "Created", "direction": "descending"}]

tools.name: notion_create_database_item
tools.description: Create a new item (row) in a Notion database with specified properties. Requires human approval.

title.description: Title for the new item (value for the title property).

properties.description: JSON string of additional properties. Example: {"Status": {"select": {"name": "To Do"}}, "Assignee": {"people": [{"id": "user-id"}]}}

content.description: Optional page body content in markdown-like format.

tools.name: notion_update_database_item
tools.description: Update an existing database item's properties (status, tags, dates, etc.).

page_id.description: The page ID of the database item to update.

properties.description: JSON string of properties to update. Example: {"Status": {"select": {"name": "Done"}}}

tools.name: notion_append_blocks
tools.description: Append content blocks to an existing Notion page. Use this to add text, headings, lists, code blocks, and other content to a page.

page_id.description: The Notion page ID to append content to.

content.description: Markdown-like content to append. Supports headings (## H2), bullets (- item), numbered lists (1. item), bold (**text**), italic (*text*), code blocks (```code```), dividers (---), and plain paragraphs.

systemPromptCapability: interact with Notion: search pages, create and update pages, query and manage databases, append content blocks via notion_* tools
